changes_summary: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.
questions: The `setIsAnnotateMode` backwards-compat setter in useInpainting (line 294) calls `setEditMode('annotate' | 'inpaint')`. After making setEditMode a plain `(mode: EditMode) => void`, this still works. But is `setIsAnnotateMode` still needed by any consumer, or can it be removed as part of this refactor? Removing it would shrink the useInpainting API surface.

success_criteria.criterion: Only one EditMode type definition exists (in editSettingsTypes.ts), with members: text | inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | upscale
No toInpaintingEditMode function exists anywhere in the codebase
No 'as ImageEditMode', 'as ImageEditState[...]', or 'as any' type casts related to editMode in touched files
useInpainting accepts editMode as a required prop (not optional) of the canonical EditMode type
useInpainting does not contain fallback editMode state (no fallbackEditMode useState)
useImageEditOrchestrator returns persistedEditMode (wide type), not inpaintingHook.editMode
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
All existing tests in src/domains/media-lightbox/ and src/tools/edit-images/ pass
No behavioral changes — all edit modes (text, inpaint, annotate, reposition, img2img, upscale) continue to work
New targeted test: orchestrator returns 'reposition' (not 'text') when persistedEditMode is 'reposition'
New targeted test: inline editor passes non-inpainting modes through without narrowing
New targeted test: useInpainting returns empty brushStrokes for non-inpainting modes (reposition, img2img, upscale)
ImageEditMode type alias is removed, replaced with EditMode | null at context boundaries
useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts uses canonical EditMode type instead of inline union
No 'enhance' string literal in src/domains/media-lightbox/ (only in video domain)
buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts has no 'as any' casts for editMode values
Manual smoke test: switch between all edit modes in lightbox and inline editor

assumptions: 'enhance' was never persisted as an image editMode value — confirmed by: (a) no code calls setEditMode('enhance') for images, (b) DEFAULT_EDIT_SETTINGS.editMode is 'text', (c) useLastUsedEditSettings defaults to 'text'. No localStorage/DB migration is needed.
The VideoEditSubMode type (which includes 'enhance') is a separate type system from EditMode and is not affected by this refactor.

GenerateButton.tsx and useRepositionGestureHandlers.ts use `editMode: string` which is type-compatible with EditMode. Tightening them to use the canonical type is optional cleanup outside this refactor's scope.
The 'magic-edit' value in buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts is not a valid EditMode and was only used with 'as any' — it can be replaced with a valid mode or removed.
